levels.title: The Loop
levels.subtitle: Understanding what you're working with
levels.description: Most people treat Claude Code like a chatbot. This level shifts your mental model: Claude Code is an autonomous agent that runs a gather, act, verify loop. You are the manager of this loop. Understanding this changes everything about how you use it.

levels.title: The Briefing
levels.subtitle: How to talk to an agent
levels.description: You are writing a briefing for a capable but context-free colleague. The quality of the briefing determines the quality of the work. Learn to define what done looks like, give Claude something to verify, and know when to reset instead of correct.

levels.title: The Dashboard
levels.subtitle: Knowing your tools
levels.description: Claude Code has a dashboard of tools, slash commands, permission modes, and session controls. You don't need to tell Claude which to use, but knowing what's available helps you understand what's possible and steer when needed.

levels.title: The Budget
levels.subtitle: Mastering context
levels.description: Context is a budget. Every file read, every message, every tool output costs context. When you overspend, quality collapses like a cliff. This is the single biggest skill gap between intermediate and expert users. Learn CLAUDE.md, memory, compaction, and strategic resets.

levels.title: The Blueprint
levels.subtitle: Planning before building
levels.description: The cheapest mistake to fix is one you never make. Plan Mode lets you explore and think before spending tokens on implementation. Learn the four-phase workflow and apply it to research, writing, data management, and debugging.

levels.title: The Workshop
levels.subtitle: Building your own tools
levels.description: Claude Code is a workshop, not a fixed tool. Build custom skills, set up automatic hooks, create specialized subagents, and connect external services through MCP. Your Claude Code becomes uniquely yours.

levels.title: The Fleet
levels.subtitle: Parallel power
levels.description: One Claude session is one worker. With worktrees, agent teams, batch mode, and CI/CD integration, you become a fleet commander coordinating multiple parallel streams of work. This is where Claude Code becomes transformatively powerful.

levels.title: The Intuition
levels.subtitle: Mastering the meta-game
levels.description: Mastery is not knowing every feature. It is having intuition for the collaboration. You know when to give Claude freedom, when to constrain it, when to reset, and when to take over. The tool becomes invisible. You just think and ship.

root.title: Getting Started
root.description: How to approach this course, who it's for, and what you'll learn at each level.

level-1.title: Level 1: The Loop
level-1.description: Understand what Claude Code actually is and how the agentic loop works.

level-1.title: 1.1 You're Not Chatting
level-1.description: Claude Code is an autonomous agent, not a chatbot. Understanding the agentic loop changes everything.

level-1.title: 1.2 Getting Started
level-1.description: Install Claude Code, authenticate, and run your first session.

level-1.title: 1.3 What You See on Screen
level-1.description: Learn to read Claude Code's visual cues — tool calls, permission prompts, and the status line.

level-1.title: 1.4 The Context Window
level-1.description: Everything Claude knows is in the context window right now. Nothing else exists.

level-1.title: 1.5 Your First Real Collaboration
level-1.description: A guided exercise where you use Claude Code to complete a real task end-to-end.

level-1.title: 1.6 Checkpoint
level-1.description: Test your understanding of the agentic loop, the interface, and the context window.

level-2.title: Level 2: The Briefing
level-2.description: Learn to communicate with Claude Code like writing a briefing for a capable colleague.

level-2.title: 2.1 The Briefing Mindset
level-2.description: Define what done looks like, not how to get there.

level-2.title: 2.2 Specificity Is Kindness
level-2.description: Precise constraints save Claude and you time. Learn the difference between vague and clear.

level-2.title: 2.3 Give Something to Verify
level-2.description: The single highest-leverage habit: include expected output, test criteria, or a reference.

level-2.title: 2.4 The Interview Pattern
level-2.description: For complex work, ask Claude to interview you first before starting.

level-2.title: 2.5 Mentions and Piping
level-2.description: Give Claude the right briefing materials with @-mentions, image paste, and piping.

level-2.title: 2.6 Knowing When to Reset
level-2.description: Correction spirals vs. starting fresh. The sunk cost trap in AI collaboration.

level-2.title: 2.7 Checkpoint
level-2.description: Rewrite bad prompts into effective briefings, then execute them.

level-3.title: Level 3: The Dashboard
level-3.description: Know every built-in tool and when to reach for it.

level-3.title: 3.1 Built-in Tools
level-3.description: Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep, Bash — what each does, in plain language.

level-3.title: 3.2 Slash Commands
level-3.description: The commands you will use daily — /clear, /compact, /context, /cost, and more.

level-3.title: 3.3 Permission Modes
level-3.description: Default, Auto-Accept, Plan Mode — choosing your trust level.

level-3.title: 3.4 Sessions
level-3.description: Picking up where you left off with --continue, --resume, /rename, and /fork.

level-3.title: 3.5 Getting Data In
level-3.description: @-mentions, image paste, piping, and other ways to give Claude information.

level-3.title: 3.6 Getting Results Out
level-3.description: /copy, /export, print mode, and structured output for getting Claude's work where you need it.

level-3.title: 3.7 The Safety Net
level-3.description: /rewind, /diff, permission prompts, and Esc — the controls that let you use Claude Code confidently.

level-3.title: 3.8 Checkpoint
level-3.description: Complete a multi-step task using at least 4 different slash commands.

level-4.title: Level 4: The Budget
level-4.description: Context is finite. Managing it is the single biggest skill gap between intermediate and expert users.

level-4.title: 4.1 Why Claude Gets Worse Over Time
level-4.description: The context cliff — quality does not degrade gradually, it falls off.

level-4.title: 4.2 What Consumes Your Budget
level-4.description: Messages, file reads, tool output, CLAUDE.md — everything has a cost.

level-4.title: 4.3 CLAUDE.md — Your Project's Always-On Briefing
level-4.description: Persistent instructions that load every session. What to include, what to leave out.

level-4.title: 4.4 The Memory System
level-4.description: Auto-memory, the rules directory, and how Claude remembers across sessions.

level-4.title: 4.5 Compaction
level-4.description: Squeezing more into the context window with /compact and auto-compaction.

level-4.title: 4.6 The /clear Strategy
level-4.description: When to throw away context and start fresh — it is strategy, not failure.

level-4.title: 4.7 Subagents as Context Shields
level-4.description: Using Explore agents to investigate without bloating your main window.

level-4.title: 4.8 Checkpoint
level-4.description: Diagnose a stale session — identify what is consuming context and fix it.

level-5.title: Level 5: The Blueprint
level-5.description: The cheapest mistake to fix is one you never make. Plan before building.

level-5.title: 5.1 Plan Mode
level-5.description: Read-only exploration — safe, cheap, and clarifying.

level-5.title: 5.2 The Four-Phase Workflow
level-5.description: Explore, Plan, Implement, Verify — the canonical pattern for non-trivial tasks.

level-5.title: 5.3 Scoping Work
level-5.description: Breaking big asks into phases Claude can handle without context overflow.

level-5.title: 5.4 Workflow: Research
level-5.description: Using Claude Code to research a topic, gather findings, and synthesize.

level-5.title: 5.5 Workflow: Writing
level-5.description: Drafting, revising, and restructuring documents with Plan Mode.

level-5.title: 5.6 Workflow: Data
level-5.description: Organizing, cleaning, and transforming data and files.

level-5.title: 5.7 Workflow: Debugging
level-5.description: The universal debug loop: reproduce, locate, understand, fix, verify.

level-5.title: 5.8 Checkpoint
level-5.description: Use the four-phase workflow on a real task from your work.

level-6.title: Level 6: The Workshop
level-6.description: Build your own tools, automations, and integrations. Claude Code becomes uniquely yours.

level-6.title: 6.1 Skills — Your Custom Commands
level-6.description: Create custom slash commands with SKILL.md files and parameterized templates.

level-6.title: 6.2 Your First Skill
level-6.description: Guided walkthrough: build a skill for a task you do repeatedly.

level-6.title: 6.3 Hooks — Automatic Reactions
level-6.description: When something happens, do something else automatically.

level-6.title: 6.4 Your First Hook
level-6.description: Guided walkthrough: build a hook that solves a real annoyance.

level-6.title: 6.5 Custom Subagents
level-6.description: Create agents with specific roles, restricted tools, and different models.

level-6.title: 6.6 MCP — Connecting to the Outside World
level-6.description: Connect external tools and services through the Model Context Protocol.

level-6.title: 6.7 Composing Your Extensions
level-6.description: How skills, hooks, agents, and MCP compose into a full ecosystem.

level-6.title: 6.8 Checkpoint
level-6.description: Build a custom workflow with at least one skill and one hook working together.

level-7.title: Level 7: The Fleet
level-7.description: Coordinate multiple parallel Claude sessions for transformative speed.

level-7.title: 7.1 Worktrees — Parallel Universes
level-7.description: Isolated git branches where Claude works independently.

level-7.title: 7.2 Agent Teams
level-7.description: Lead plus teammates with parallel investigation and task assignment.

level-7.title: 7.3 Batch Mode — Fan Out
level-7.description: Apply the same change across many files at once with /batch.

level-7.title: 7.4 Non-Interactive Automation
level-7.description: Using claude -p in scripts with JSON output, turn limits, and budget controls.

level-7.title: 7.5 CI/CD Integration
level-7.description: Running Claude Code in GitHub Actions and automated pipelines.

level-7.title: 7.6 The Agent SDK
level-7.description: Python and TypeScript SDK for programmatic agent control.

level-7.title: 7.7 Remote and Mobile Access
level-7.description: Remote sessions, teleport, mobile access, and the desktop app.

level-7.title: 7.8 Checkpoint
level-7.description: Set up an automated workflow that runs Claude Code without you in the loop.

level-8.title: Level 8: The Intuition
level-8.description: Mastery is intuition for the collaboration. The tool becomes invisible.

level-8.title: 8.1 Patterns That Matter
level-8.description: All the mental models synthesized into intuitive behavior.

level-8.title: 8.2 When Not to Use Claude
level-8.description: Tasks better done manually and the trap of automation theater.

level-8.title: 8.3 When Claude Breaks
level-8.description: Loops, hallucinations, misunderstandings — recovery patterns and when to take over.

level-8.title: 8.4 Evolving Your Setup
level-8.description: CLAUDE.md pruning, skill iteration, and reviewing your usage patterns.

level-8.title: 8.5 Settings Architecture
level-8.description: User, project, local, and managed settings — precedence rules and team sharing.

level-8.title: 8.6 Teaching Others
level-8.description: How to onboard teammates and build team-wide standards.

level-8.title: 8.7 Final Capstone
level-8.description: End-to-end project proving your patterns of thought transfer to any domain.
